description:
Legal document. Record of release; partnership renewal. Written in the hand of Hillel b. ʿEli (fl. 1066–1108 CE). These two fragments are part of single document containing a partnership release and renewal. Amat al-Qādir, widow of Shela, and her son Yefet (possibly a minor), release ʿArūs b. Yosef from a partnership he had with Shela. 

Amat al-Qādir seems to be acting as trustee for her son, but also seems to have a financial interest in the partnership; since she would not have ordinarily inherited from her husband, her equity in the partnership may represent payment of her ketubba. The widow and son are to receive two thirds of the profits from the investment, and ʿArūs is to receive the balance "as a claim for his service". Following the commenda model, ʿArūs assumes no responsibility for loss. ʿArūs is to travel between Fusṭāṭ and Yemen with the funds and has discretion in choosing the commodities he trades; he promises that he will work "with caution ... as if [he] were working with [his] own assets." (Information in part from Lieberman, "A Partnership Culture," 122.) Join: Alan Elbaum. 

On verso there is an unsigned legal declaration in Arabic script in which Ḥasan b. Sahl (a.k.a. Yefet b. Shela, the same as in the document on recto) declares that he has received 119 dinars from his mother on the aforementioned date, either 47[.] AH = 1078–87 CE or 49[.] AH = 1097–1106 CE.

This document was mistakenly identified as a join with T-S 10J5.2 (PGPID 40179) in Goitein, Med Soc III (viii,C,3, n. 24), and it was also treated as such in Lieberman, "A Partnership Culture," 122. In fact, these are two copies of the same document.
description_he: מסמך משפטי ביהודית-ערבית מהמאה ה-11. מוחזק בספריית קיימברידג'.
